problem.title: Sum of Two Integers
problem.description: Given two integers a and b, return the sum of the two integers without using the operators + and -.
assumptions: Use bitwise XOR and AND with shift.

example.explanation: a+b without + or -. XOR gives sum without carry; AND<<1 gives carry. Repeat until no carry.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Bit Manipulation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: XOR computes the digit-wise sum without carries, while AND followed by a left-shift computes the carry bits; iterating until there are no more carries yields the full sum. This works because binary addition is just repeated XOR with carry propagation. The key insight is that AND captures every position where both bits are 1 (a carry must be generated) and left-shifting moves those carries to the next more-significant bit.
